batch_1_4_summary.root_cause: HANDS execution latency — LinkedIn window (Day 3-6) missed on every batch of candidates across all 29 campaign days
batch_1_4_summary.channel_verdict: Architecturally ready. Operationally unexecuted. The BRAIN intelligence layer functioned correctly; the HANDS execution layer did not execute on any of 9 consecutive runs.

batch_5_revised_protocol.mandatory_same_session_rule: HANDS must search, check posts, and send note in ONE SESSION on Day 3, 4, 5, or 6. Do not split across days.
batch_5_revised_protocol.brain_flagging: BRAIN will mark LinkedIn send days with MANDATORY flag in daily_signals.json.
batch_5_revised_protocol.profiles_json_requirement: HANDS must write linkedin_profiles.json immediately after each search session — whether or not a note was sent.
batch_5_revised_protocol.target_turnaround: Within 2 hours of BRAIN run on Day 3-6. Maximum: same calendar day.
batch_5_revised_protocol.fallback_escalation: If HANDS cannot execute within the window: log in CHANGELOG.md with reason. Do not skip silently.
batch_5_revised_protocol.weekend_rule: LinkedIn Day 3-6 window that falls on a Saturday or Sunday: hold to Monday. Exception: if Day 6 is Sunday and Day 7 fires Monday — send Sunday 18:00+.
